prompt: A reviewer question ('what if values is empty?') on average() is really a ZeroDivisionError defect; a second comment is pure praise. The skill should investigate, fix the crash with a Conventional-Commits-style commit, reply to the question citing a bare short SHA, and post NO reply to the praise. Don't post or push.

assertions.text: average() is guarded against an empty input so it no longer raises ZeroDivisionError (e.g. early return of 0 or None when len(values) == 0)
The fix is committed and the commit subject follows Conventional Commits style (e.g. starts with `fix(` or `feat(`) to match the repo history
The reply to the question cites a short SHA as bare text, not wrapped in backticks, and frames it as a real bug that was fixed
The praise comment receives NO drafted reply (restraint), or is explicitly noted as needing none
No tooling chatter or AI self-reference; gh is not invoked and the branch is not pushed
